description_long: This object is a pair of metal pliers with a dark, weathered finish, likely made from steel or iron. The handles are long and slightly curved, providing leverage for gripping and manipulating objects. The jaws are robust and feature a serrated gripping surface, with a central notch for cutting or holding wires. The pivot joint is a classic fixed type, allowing the two arms to rotate around a single axis. The surface of the handles appears uncoated and lacks any visible ergonomic padding or insulation. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the tool, and the overall appearance suggests it is a utilitarian, possibly older or well-used hand tool.
description: A weathered, dark metal pair of pliers with curved handles and serrated jaws, featuring a central notch for gripping or cutting. The tool has a utilitarian, uncoated finish and no visible branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handles and the serrated jaws with a central notch.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth of the jaws and the curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handles' curve and the robust, utilitarian design of the jaws.
description_view_3: Front-angled view focusing on the jaw opening and the pivot joint, with the handles receding into the background.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, uninsulated metal handles with a simple cylindrical profile.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, robust jaws with a flat, serrated gripping surface and a central notch for wire cutting or holding.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single-axis pivot joint, non-adjustable, classic pliers design.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible ergonomic or textured grip pattern.
